titleStmt.title: Letter from Joseph Devlin to Margaret O'Brien, 12 May 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Joseph Devlin

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from Joseph Devlin to Margaret O'Brien regarding the incorrect internment of Margaret's husband's remains. According to Devlin the remains are on grounds not provided for by the Public Health Act and for that reason the application should be made to Lord Lieutenant, Ivor Guest (1873-1939).

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/5613/9083/2

correspAction.placeName: The Green, Midleton, Co. Cork, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
LOCAL GOVERNMENT BOARD, DUBLIN. 12th May, 1916.
No. 21,438 : 1916,
Midleston Urban District. Madam, I am directed by the Local Government Board for
Ireland to acknowledge the receipt of your letter of
the 26th ultimo, and, in reply I am to state that if,
as would appear to be the case, the remains of your
late husband are interred in a Burial Ground not proâ
vided under the Public Health Acts, you should address
your application for the disinterment of the remains to
His Excellency the Lord Lieutenant. I am,
Madam,
Your obedient Servant, Joseph Devlin
Assistant Secretary. Mrs Margaret O'Brien, The Green, Midleton.
